config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society is important, but it should not automatically come before the rights of the individual, because that can easily become a justification for abuse and injustice. In my view, the best society is one that protects basic individual rights while still asking people to contribute to the common good. From my perspective, collective interests matter, but they should have clear limits.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, social stability and collective welfare are very important, and I understand why many people place them high, but I have also seen how easily “the greater good” can be used to silence ordinary people or excuse unfair treatment. A healthy society should protect basic individual rights first, because once those rights are ignored, the society itself becomes less trustworthy and less just. I would put the relationship as a balance, not a hierarchy, with clear limits on what society can demand from the individual.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take automatic priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in Indonesia, I understand the appeal of harmony, gotong royong, and public order, but I also know how quickly “for society” can be used to pressure people into silence or unfair treatment. A good society protects individual rights as the baseline, then asks people to contribute to the common good within those limits. If society’s interests can override rights too easily, then the rights are not real protections at all.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual, because once that rule is accepted, it becomes too easy for authorities or majorities to excuse harm in the name of harmony or stability. As someone from China, I understand why people value collective welfare, social order, and long-term public goals, but I have also seen how “for the greater good” can be used to pressure ordinary people and weaken basic protections. To me, a society is only truly strong when individual rights are treated as the bottom line, not as something society can suspend whenever it finds it convenient. Collective interests matter, but they should be pursued within firm limits, not above the individual.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should never automatically outrank the rights of the individual. From my perspective in Indonesia, I understand the value of harmony, gotong royong, and public order, but I also know that “for the common good” can be used too easily to pressure people into silence or unfair treatment. The strongest society is not the one that asks individuals to sacrifice their rights first; it is the one that protects those rights as a baseline while still encouraging responsibility to others. Society matters, but if individual rights can be overridden whenever it is convenient, then they are not real rights at all.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual, because once that principle is accepted, it is too easy for power to excuse injustice in the name of harmony or stability. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why collective welfare and social order are valued, and I do not dismiss them, but I have also seen how “for the greater good” can pressure ordinary people and weaken real protections. A society is stronger when it treats basic rights as the bottom line and asks people to contribute within clear limits. If individual rights can be suspended whenever society finds it convenient, then those rights are not genuine rights at all.
